methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Epoch Biodesign is a Series A biotech company founded by Jacob Nathan that has raised $40M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including Series B-stage peers such as Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $40M total raised as a baseline and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, implies a valuation of $80M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $805M, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. The public-comp anchoring method is not applicable, as Epoch's revenue is not on file; the three public Biotech & Health comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) show a median P/S multiple of 6.6x, but cannot be applied without the target's revenue figure.

Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $68M to $926M, with a median of $442M. The wide dispersion between stage-progression ($80M) and peer-median ($805M) reflects the heterogeneity of Series B valuations in the comp set and uncertainty regarding Epoch's relative technology maturity, IP position, and commercial readiness versus peer benchmarks. Key uncertainties include revenue traction, clinical/regulatory stage, and capital intensity of the underlying platform.
